pages.raw_text:
As soon as Commissioners ]. T.
O'Brien can corral representatives
of the Washington Water Power
Co., a contract for lighting the city,
as authorized by the council last
night, will be made For the past
two weeks the commissioners have
had trouble with the company over
a clause designed to give the COM
pany exclusive right to the privi-
lege. Finally the corporation coun-
sci was appealed to and rendered
an opinion that 1t was unlawful for
the city to grant an exclusive
privilege to anyone. Fortified with
this opinion, O'Brien last evening
met representative of the COM
pany and they agreed that the
clause should be left out, thus CIV
ing competing company, when
ever ready to enter the field, a
chance to bid on additional lights.


1t is stated that representatives
of the Traction Co. were instru-
mental in holding up the contract
